headline: Drone Strikes Test AWS Resilience And Create New Questions For Amazon Investors
summary: Drone attacks hit three Amazon Web Services data centers in the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain. The strikes caused structural damage, service outages, and disruptions for customers in affected regions. AWS advised clients to shift workloads to other regions to maintain continuity. This is the first instance of a major U.S. tech company’s cloud infrastructure being directly disrupted by military action. For investors watching Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN), the incident highlights the...

headline: Iran Strikes Shatter the Gulf’s Post-Oil Pivot
summary: The Gulf’s costly gamble to build a post-oil future is now facing trial by combat.  After years—and hundreds of billions of dollars—spent transforming the region into a nexus for artificial intelligence, tourism and logistics, those aspirations are now under fire as Iranian strikes pound the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia and neighboring states.  Amazon  said early Tuesday it expected extended service interruptions following damage from drone attacks on three data centers in the region.

After years—and hundreds of billions of dollars—spent transforming the region into a nexus for artificial intelligence, tourism and logistics, those aspirations are now under fire as Iranian strikes pound the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia and neighboring states.  Amazon  said early Tuesday it expected extended service interruptions following damage from drone attacks on three data centers in the region.

headline: Amazon launches 15 minute grocery delivery in 'priority' market Brazil
summary: Amazon launched its Amazon Now service in Brazil on Tuesday, pledging to deliver essentials and groceries in 15 minutes, ‌with its country head saying the Latin American country is the ‌firm's top priority for new investments.  The service will launch in Sao Paulo on Tuesday, expanding  gradually ​to seven other cities by March 9, Amazon Brasil's shopping experience director Fernanda Grumach told a press conference, adding that there are plans for a broad expansion later.  Brazil's e-commerce segment has seen growing competition in recent years between players like Amazon, Uruguay-based ‌MercadoLibre and Singapore firm ⁠Sea's Shopee.

Brazil's e-commerce segment has seen growing competition in recent years between players like Amazon, Uruguay-based ‌MercadoLibre and Singapore firm ⁠Sea's Shopee.

headline: Tech Weekly: Amazon data centers hit by drones, AI takes over MWC
summary: STORY: From drones striking Amazon data centres to AI taking the Mobile World Congress by storm.This is Tech Weekly.:: Tech WeeklyAmazon data centers in the UAE and Bahrain were damaged by drone strikes from the Middle East conflict, the company said on Monday.Cloud services have been disrupted with the tech giant stating the recovery will be "prolonged."It marks the first time a major U.S. tech company's data center has been disrupted by military action.The strikes raise questions around Big Tech's pace of expansion in the region.New legislation could see Poland ban social media for children under 15 years of age.The government says platforms will be responsible for age verification, or face fines.Several European governments have explored similar restrictions amid claims social-media services are harmful or addictive for minors.AI technology and next generation robotics were the focus for the World Mobile Congress in Barcelona.Exhibitors are showcasing how AI is reshaping smartphones, wearables and connected services.AI and the ethics surrounding its rapid deployment sits high on this year’s agenda.SpaceX launched a new batch of Starlink satellites into low‑Earth orbit, the company said on Sunday.Twenty-nine internet providing satellites were successfully released, completing the company’s 22nd Starlink mission of the year.The Elon Musk's owned company is preparing a blockbuster public listing later this year that could value it at over $1.5 trillion.Sportswear brand On Running has opened a factory in South Korea using robots to make running shoes.Co-founder Caspar Coppetti says automation enables the brand to make shoes faster.The Swiss company said it plans to open similar factories in the United States and Europe.Tariff hikes, supply chain disruptions and geopolitical risks are driving brands to manufacture their products closer to the end consumer.

raw.headline: Tech Weekly: Amazon data centers hit by drones, AI takes over MWC
raw.summary: STORY: From drones striking Amazon data centres to AI taking the Mobile World Congress by storm.This is Tech Weekly.:: Tech WeeklyAmazon data centers in the UAE and Bahrain were damaged by drone strikes from the Middle East conflict, the company said on Monday.Cloud services have been disrupted with the tech giant stating the recovery will be "prolonged."It marks the first time a major U.S. tech company's data center has been disrupted by military action.The strikes raise questions around Big Tech's pace of expansion in the region.New legislation could see Poland ban social media for children under 15 years of age.The government says platforms will be responsible for age verification, or face fines.Several European governments have explored similar restrictions amid claims social-media services are harmful or addictive for minors.AI technology and next generation robotics were the focus for the World Mobile Congress in Barcelona.Exhibitors are showcasing how AI is reshaping smartphones, wearables and connected services.AI and the ethics surrounding its rapid deployment sits high on this year’s agenda.SpaceX launched a new batch of Starlink satellites into low‑Earth orbit, the company said on Sunday.Twenty-nine internet providing satellites were successfully released, completing the company’s 22nd Starlink mission of the year.The Elon Musk's owned company is preparing a blockbuster public listing later this year that could value it at over $1.5 trillion.Sportswear brand On Running has opened a factory in South Korea using robots to make running shoes.Co-founder Caspar Coppetti says automation enables the brand to make shoes faster.The Swiss company said it plans to open similar factories in the United States and Europe.Tariff hikes, supply chain disruptions and geopolitical risks are driving brands to manufacture their products closer to the end consumer.

headline: New York Pension Wants Data Centers to Be Good Neighbors
summary: Pension funds are joining the crusade to shield communities from the noisy, energy-guzzling impact of AI data centers.  The New York State Common Retirement Fund wrote to Amazon, Meta Platforms, Alphabet and 15 other tech companies, asking them to prioritize the needs of communities where they own, operate, or lease data centers.  In a letter, it said this was to “protect shareholder value and ensure sustainable growth.”

The New York State Common Retirement Fund wrote to Amazon, Meta Platforms, Alphabet and 15 other tech companies, asking them to prioritize the needs of communities where they own, operate, or lease data centers.  In a letter, it said this was to “protect shareholder value and ensure sustainable growth.”

headline: Drone Strikes Damage Amazon Data Centers in the UAE and Bahrain
summary: Amazon Web Services Inc. said for the first time that drones had “directly struck” two facilities in the UAE.  “We are working to restore full service availability as quickly as possible, though we expect recovery to be prolonged given the nature of the physical damage involved,” the company said in a post.  AWS customers are experiencing elevated error rates and degraded availability, the company said.

AWS customers are experiencing elevated error rates and degraded availability, the company said.
